biodynamic.name: Chateau Pontet-Canet
biodynamic.description: The pioneering biodynamic estate in Pauillac, converting all 81 hectares to Demeter-certified biodynamic farming in 2014 under Alfred Tesseron and technical director Mathieu Bessonnet. Horse-drawn ploughing, composts, and biodynamic preparations used across the full estate.

biodynamic.name: Chateau Palmer
biodynamic.description: Margaux third growth achieving full Demeter biodynamic certification across all 66 hectares in 2017, making it the largest biodynamic classified growth in the Medoc. Thomas Duroux's meticulous conversion includes 100% estate-produced compost, lunar calendar planning and no herbicides since 2008.

biodynamic.name: Chateau Lafleur (Biodynamic Practicing)
biodynamic.certifier: Ecocert (organic certification); biodynamic practices without Demeter certification
biodynamic.description: Baptiste Guinaudeau converted Chateau Lafleur to organic farming certified by Ecocert and to biodynamic practices from 2014. While not Demeter-certified, the estate applies full biodynamic preparations and lunar scheduling. Tiny 4.5-hectare Pomerol estate with equal Merlot-Cabernet Franc.

organic.name: Chateau Lafite Rothschild (Ecocert Organic)
organic.description: Under Saskia de Rothschild's leadership, Chateau Lafite completed a full organic conversion of all 112 hectares, receiving Ecocert certification in 2024. The conversion began in 2016; Lafite becomes the first first-growth to achieve full organic certification.

organic.name: Chateau Cos d'Estournel (Ecocert Organic)
organic.description: Saint-Estephe second growth Cos d'Estournel achieved Ecocert organic certification across all 91 hectares in 2024 under owner Michel Reybier's sustainability mandate and direct management.

organic.name: Chateau Smith Haut Lafitte (Ecocert Organic)
organic.address: Chateau Smith Haut Lafitte, 33650 Martillac, France
organic.description: The Cathiard family's Pessac-Leognan estate and Les Sources de Caudalie resort are Ecocert organic certified. The 67-hectare vineyard uses cover crops, organic composts and no systemic herbicides; the estate's sustainability programme extends to the Vinotherapie spa's grape-based treatments.

natural.name: Chateau Roc de Cambes (Low-Intervention Cotes de Bourg)
natural.address: Chateau Roc de Cambes, 33710 Bourg-sur-Gironde, France
natural.description: Francois Mitjavile's Cotes de Bourg estate is widely cited by natural wine importers as one of Bordeaux's most compelling low-intervention wines: no herbicides, hand-harvesting, gravity-flow cellaring, and minimal sulfite addition. Not certified but well documented in natural wine circles.

natural.name: Wine Not Bar (Bordeaux Natural Wine Showcase)
natural.sulfite_addition: low to zero - producers on the list work at less than 50mg/l total SO2
natural.description: Wine Not Bar in Saint-Michel serves the best curated natural Bordeaux wine list in the city, with producers from Fronsac, Cotes de Bourg, Entre-Deux-Mers, and Pessac-Leognan who practice low-sulfite, hand-harvested, unfined and unfiltered winemaking.

vegan_winemaking.name: Chateau Figeac (Vegan-Friendly)
vegan_winemaking.fining_method: Pea protein and bentonite; no animal-derived fining agents confirmed from 2020 vintage
vegan_winemaking.description: Chateau Figeac switched to pea protein and bentonite fining from the 2020 vintage, eliminating egg white and isinglass from its winemaking process. The estate's Ecocert organic certification and the equal-thirds Bordeaux blend make it a vegan-friendly Premier Grand Cru Classe A choice.

lowsulfite.name: Chateau Pontet-Canet (Low Sulfite)
lowsulfite.sulfite_range: Total SO2 typically below 60mg/l; well below legal Bordeaux maximum of 150mg/l for reds
lowsulfite.description: Demeter-certified Pontet-Canet maintains very low sulfite additions as part of its biodynamic programme, using native yeasts, minimal intervention winemaking, and lower-sulfite wine stability to produce wines well below the legal maximum for Bordeaux rouge.

lowsulfite.name: Chateau Palmer (Low Sulfite)
lowsulfite.sulfite_range: Total SO2 typically 50-70mg/l; natural yeast fermentation without sulfur additions at crushing
lowsulfite.description: Chateau Palmer's biodynamic conversion eliminated sulfur additions at crushing and reduced total sulfite levels significantly. Native yeasts and the biodynamic preparation programme contribute to wine stability without conventional sulfite additions at harvest.
